title: Perdure Guard Outcome
description: The outcome of `perdure guard commit` or `perdure guard abort`. A commit finalizes a verified, unchanged-since-verify session into Perdure's own ledger only — it does not create a git commit or touch git in any way. When `ok` is false, `reason` explains the refusal (not verified, tree changed since verify, or out-of-scope writes present) and the run is left resumable so the agent can fix and retry.

ok.description: True when the operation finalized the session; false when it was refused.

reason.description: Why a commit was refused, or null on success.

status.description: The run's status after the operation: completed, cancelled, or still running on a refusal.

phase.description: Session phase after the operation: committed, aborted, or unchanged on a refusal.
